analogy: Think of "Molecular Basis of Inheritance" through the chapter's own opening images: 110 BIOLOGY Evolutionary Biology is the study of history of life forms on earth. To understand the changes in flora and fauna that have occurred over millions of years on earth, we must have an understanding of the context of origin of life, i.e., evolution... The easy idea is to follow the chapter's own clues one by one instead of memorising a loose definition. First notice the words and examples the text repeats: BIOLOGY, 6.1 ORIGIN OF LIFE, CHAPTER 6, EVOLUTION. Then connect each example to the question the chapter is trying to answer. Like solving a source-backed puzzle, every useful point must come from the chapter text itself and stay traceable to the NCERT source.
full_notes:
Molecular Basis of Inheritance is decoded here only from the NCERT chapter text. The chapter begins with its own framing, examples and questions, then builds the topic through source-backed facts. The notes below keep that order: first the opening problem, then the important terms, then the mechanisms, examples and exam-useful connections that appear in the chapter itself.

The text explains that 110 BIOLOGY Evolutionary Biology is the study of history of life forms on earth.

The text explains that to understand the changes in flora and fauna that have occurred over millions of years on earth, we must have an understanding of the context of origin of life, i.e., evolution of earth, of stars and indeed of the universe itself.

The text explains that what follows is the longest of all the construed and conjectured stories.

The text explains that this is the story of origin of life and evolution of life forms or biodiversity on planet earth in the context of evolution of earth and against the background of evolution of universe itself.

The text explains that 6.1 ORIGIN OF LIFE When we look at stars on a clear night sky we are, in a way, looking back in time.

The text explains that stellar distances are measured in light years.

The text explains that what we see today is an object whose emitted light started its journey millions of year back and from trillions of kilometres away and reaching our eyes now.

The text explains that however, when we see objects in our immediate surroundings we see them instantly and hence in the present time.

The text explains that therefore, when we see stars we apparently are peeping into the past.

The text explains that the origin of life is considered a unique event in the history of universe.

The text explains that relatively speaking the earth itself is almost only a speck.

The text explains that the universe is very CHAPTER 6 EVOLUTION 6.1 Origin of Life 6.2 Evolution of Life Forms - A Theory 6.3 What are the Evidences for Evolution?

The text explains that 6.5 Biological Evolution 6.6 Mechanism of Evolution 6.7 Hardy - Weinberg Principle 6.8 A Brief Account of Evolution 6.9 Origin and Evolution of Man

The text explains that 111 EVOLUTION old – almost 13.8 billion years old.

The text explains that huge clusters of galaxies comprise the universe.

The text explains that galaxies contain stars and clouds of gas and dust.

The text explains that considering the size of universe, earth is indeed a speck.

The text explains that the Big Bang theory attempts to explain to us the origin of universe.

The text explains that the universe expanded and hence, the temperature came down.

The text explains that in the solar system of the milky way galaxy, earth was supposed to have been formed about 4.5 billion years back.

The text explains that life appeared 500 million years after the formation of earth, i.e., almost four billion years back.

The text explains that early Greek thinkers thought units of life called spores were transferred to different planets including earth. ‘Panspermia’ is still a favourite idea for some astronomers.

The text explains that however, this did not answer how the first life form came on earth.

The text explains that the conditions on earth were – high temperature, volcanic storms, reducing atmosphere containing CH4, NH3, etc.

The text explains that analysis of meteorite content also revealed similar compounds indicating that similar processes are occurring elsewhere in space.

The text explains that there had been extinctions of different life forms in the years gone by just as new forms of life arose at different periods of history of earth.

The text explains that however, these ancestors were present at different periods in the history of earth (epochs, periods and eras).

The text explains that the geological history of earth closely correlates with the biological history of earth.

The text explains that a common permissible conclusion is that earth is very old, not thousand of years as was thought earlier but billions of years old.

The text explains that evidence that evolution of life forms has indeed taken place on earth has come from many quarters.

The text explains that fossils are remains of hard parts of life-forms found in rocks.

The text explains that rocks form sediments and a cross-section of earth's crust indicates the arrangement of sediments one over the other during the long history of earth.

The text explains that different-aged rock sediments contain fossils of different life-forms who probably died during the formation of the particular sediment.

The text explains that hence, new forms of life have arisen at different times in the history of earth.

The text explains that embryological support for evolution was also proposed by Ernst Heckel based upon the observation of certain features during embryonic stage common to all vertebrates that are absent in adult.

The text explains that though these forelimbs perform different functions in these animals, they have similar anatomical structure – all of them have humerus, radius, ulna, carpals, metacarpals and phalanges in their forelimbs.

The text explains that hence, in these animals, the same structure developed along different directions due to adaptations to different needs.

The text explains that this is divergent evolution and these structures are homologous.

The text explains that they are not anatomically similar structures though they perform similar functions.

The text explains that hence, analogous structures are a result of convergent evolution - different structures evolving for the same function and hence having similarity.

The text explains that one can say that it is the similar habitat that has resulted in selection of similar adaptive features in different groups of organisms but toward the same function: Sweet potato (root modification) and potato (stem modification) is another example for analogy.

The text explains that this also tells us that evolution is not a directed process in the sense of determinism.

The text explains that it is a stochastic process based on chance events in nature and chance mutation in the organisms.

The text explains that from the original seed-eating features, many other forms with altered beaks arose, enabling them to become insectivorous and vegetarian finches (Figure 6.5).

The text explains that this process of evolution of different species in a given geographical area starting from a point and literally radiating to other areas of geography (habitats) is called adaptive radiation.

The text explains that 6.5 BIOLOGICAL EVOLUTION Evolution by natural selection, in a true sense would have started when cellular forms of life with differences in metabolic capability originated on earth.

The text explains that using various resources such as your school Library or the internet and discussions with your teacher, trace the evolutionary stages of any one animal, say horse.

The text explains that sUMMARY The origin of life on earth can be understood only against the background of origin of universe especially earth.

The text explains that most scientists believe chemical evolution, i.e., formation of biomolecules preceded the appearance of the first cellular forms of life.

The text explains that the subsequent events as to what happened to the first form of life is a conjectured story based on Darwinian ideas of organic evolution by natural selection.

The text explains that diversity of life forms on earth has been changing over millions of years.

The text explains that it is generally believed that variations in a population result in variable fitness.

Source: NCERT Class 12 Biology, Chapter 6: Molecular Basis of Inheritance

schemes.point: This intensive breeding programme has created breeds that differ from other breeds (e.g., dogs) but still are of the same group. (NCERT Class 12 Biology Ch6 pg 5)

prelims_traps: Do not treat BIOLOGY and 6.1 ORIGIN OF LIFE as identical unless the source text links them directly.
Do not add outside examples to Molecular Basis of Inheritance; use only the examples and processes named in the chapter.
Do not confuse a visible surface result with the internal process or source evidence described by the text.
mains_angles: Explain Molecular Basis of Inheritance through the source chain: evidence, process and visible result.
Use BIOLOGY and 6.1 ORIGIN OF LIFE to show how the chapter connects facts with mechanisms.
Frame an answer around causes, internal working, surface expression and source limitations from the NCERT chapter.
